PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar Jul-12-2022 Continued To Jul-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Walter Cook, via Zoom, for Raphael Taliaferro, Personal Representative.

Mr. Cook reports that regrettably, this estate has no interest in the money escheated to the State of California as previously reported to the court and requests to strike the request to withdraw filed on Jul-07-2022 and requests that the court close the estate for lack of funds. The court advises counsel that it will be continuing the Petition to Terminate, etc., for two weeks to allow the Examiner time to review this matter.

The matter is continued to Jul-26-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Continued To Jan-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Walter Cook appeared by zoom for Petitioner.

Mr. Cook stated that the Taliaferro's found no assets for Raphael and that he will file a petition with an indication of no assets in the estate. The Court informed Mr. Cook that it needs to be a petition to terminate estate for lack of assets. Status on close of estate is continued to January 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Continued To Oct-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Walter Cook to appear on October 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and why the final inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Walter Cook for Petitioner.
Charlotte Crawford appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that it did not receive any objections from Ms. Crawford. Ms. Crawford requested additional time stating that she was unsuccessful in securing someone to help her that would be affordable and asked if pro bono mediation could be explored. The Court stated that mediation could be an option down the road, but they need to get an Administrator appointed to handle the estate. Mr. Cook stated that they need to do some investigation, that he will be happy to do mediation, but they need to investigate what has happened and what the assets are, and that the special letters expire today. Mr. Cook also stated that the authority granted to his Client was very limited, that if given full authority they could do an 850 petition and subpoena records, that they will do a broad investigation as to what happened to the assets and do some discovery, and that they believe there is seven figures in assets out there somewhere. The Court stated that this matter has been pending since June, that Ms. Crawford was given five weeks to find Counsel, that Ms. Crawford did not provide any information to the Court, that it is inclined to grant the petition as the estate needs someone in place to be administered and noted that there is no counter petition. Mr. Cook stated that they only found that $2,500.00 had been removed and that the Court can limit his Client's ability. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with limited authority (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $20,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for September 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encouraged Ms. Crawford to continue her search for Counsel, to remain active in the case and to pursue her interest.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-07-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Walter Cook for and with Raphael Taliaferro.
Charlotte Crawford appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. Crawford stated that she has not filed anything. Mr. Taliaferro stated that Anthony did not have any children. Mr. Cook stated that no assets have been discovered at this time and that his Client needs letters to do that. Ms. Crawford stated that she e-mailed a copy of the marriage certificate to Mr. Cook yesterday and that the Petitioner did not have a relationship with the Father at all. The Court informed Ms. Crawford that if she is objecting to the petition she needs to submit them in writing and that it can continue the matter for her to do so. Ms. Crawford stated that she would like to do that, that documents were sent to an address where she is no longer staying, provided Mr. Cook with her phone number, stated that she is in the process of determining where she is going to live and asked for time to get an Attorney. Mr. Cook stated that he had not received anything from Ms. Crawford, provided Ms. Crawford with two fax numbers and suggested that his Client be appointed with limited powers for the purpose of obtaining bank records. Ms. Crawford is to provide Mr. Cook with a mailing address for notice. The Court granted special administration to Raphael Taliaferro with limited authority (subject to receipt and review of the order). A minimum bond in the amount of $20,000.00 is required. The Court reminds Mr. Taliaferro and Counsel that if they discover more assets, it is their duty to come in to have the bond increased to an appropriate amount. The Petition for Letters Testamentary is continued to September 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Crawford is to have her objections filed by September 11, 2019.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Siu. Not reported.
